title: [28.x] Backports
summary: This is a backport PR that brings four previously merged changes to the 28.x release branch. The specific changes being backported are referenced by their PR numbers.
potential_rationale: This likely helps maintain the 28.x release branch by incorporating important fixes or improvements that were merged into the main development branch, ensuring users on the stable release get critical updates.

title: Update leveldb subtree to latest upstream
summary: Updates Bitcoin Core's embedded leveldb database library to the latest upstream version. This includes three specific changes from the leveldb-subtree repository.
potential_rationale: This may help improve database performance, fix potential bugs, or add security improvements from the upstream leveldb project, which could enhance Bitcoin Core's blockchain storage reliability and efficiency.

title: leveldb: pull upstream C++23 changes
summary: Cherry-picks two commits from upstream leveldb that remove usage of deprecated C++ features (std::aligned_storage/std::aligned_union) to maintain C++23 compatibility.
potential_rationale: This likely ensures Bitcoin Core remains compatible with newer C++ standards and compilers, which may help with future maintenance, security, and performance as the C++ ecosystem evolves.

title: guix: remove test-security/symbol-check scripts
summary: Removes the test-security and symbol-check scripts from the Guix build system. The author argues these scripts have become more problematic than helpful since adopting Guix for release builds.
potential_rationale: This appears to streamline the build process by removing tests that may have become redundant with Guix's deterministic build environment, potentially reducing build complexity and maintenance burden.

title: doc: swap CPPFLAGS for APPEND_CPPFLAGS
summary: Updates documentation to replace CPPFLAGS with APPEND_CPPFLAGS in examples. The current documentation shows a variable that CMake doesn't recognize, leading to warning messages.
potential_rationale: This likely helps developers and users who follow the documentation avoid confusion and build warnings when using CMake, which may improve the overall developer experience and reduce support burden.

title: build: consistently use `CLIENT_NAME` in libbitcoinkernel.pc.in
summary: Updates the libbitcoinkernel.pc.in file to consistently use the CLIENT_NAME variable instead of hardcoded values. This is a follow-up fix to ensure proper naming consistency in the pkg-config file.
potential_rationale: This change likely helps maintain consistent branding and naming across Bitcoin Core's build artifacts, which may be important for downstream projects that depend on libbitcoinkernel and use pkg-config to discover build information.

title: depends: add missing Darwin objcopy
summary: Adds the missing Darwin objcopy tool reference to the CMake toolchain configuration. Currently the toolchain file references a non-existent objcopy tool for Darwin cross-compilation builds.
potential_rationale: This change appears to improve the correctness of the build system by ensuring all toolchain references point to actual tools, which may prevent potential build failures or confusion for developers working on Darwin cross-compilation, even though objcopy isn't currently used in Darwin builds.

title: depends: set `CMAKE_*_COMPILER_TARGET` in toolchain
summary: Sets the CMAKE_*_COMPILER_TARGET variable in the toolchain file to follow CMake's recommended approach for cross-compilation with Clang. This prevents duplicated target flags on the compiler command line.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves build reliability and follows CMake best practices, which may help prevent compilation issues and ensure more predictable cross-compilation behavior, particularly for Apple platform builds where duplicate target flags could cause problems.

title: ci: switch MSAN to use prebuilt Clang binaries
summary: Changes the Memory Sanitizer (MSAN) CI configuration to use prebuilt Clang binaries from apt.llvm.org instead of building from source. This updates the testing infrastructure to use packaged binaries.
potential_rationale: This change likely improves CI build times and reliability by using maintained binary packages instead of compiling Clang from source, which may reduce CI infrastructure costs and make the testing process more stable and faster for memory sanitizer checks.

title: depends: avoid an unset `CMAKE_OBJDUMP`
summary: Fixes the Linux toolchain file to properly set CMAKE_OBJDUMP instead of leaving it as an empty string. This ensures consistency with other toolchain variables that point to actual cross-compilation tools.
potential_rationale: This change appears to improve toolchain file completeness and consistency, which may help prevent potential build issues or confusion for developers doing Linux cross-compilation, ensuring all expected tools are properly referenced even if not currently used.

title: doc: add missing copyright headers
summary: Adds missing copyright headers to various files and removes some obsolete documentation and code.
potential_rationale: This likely helps ensure proper legal compliance and code attribution, which may be important for a project like Bitcoin Core that needs clear licensing. Removing obsolete content appears to help maintain code cleanliness.

title: build: move `rpc/external_signer` to node library
summary: Moves the RPC external signer functionality from the common library to the node library and removes a dependency check suppression.
potential_rationale: This appears to improve the organization of code modules by placing external signer RPC functionality in a more appropriate library location. This may help with build efficiency and clearer separation of concerns in the codebase.

title: build: remove ENABLE_HARDENING condition from check-security
summary: Removes a conditional check that would skip security hardening verification in release builds.
potential_rationale: This likely strengthens security by ensuring that hardening checks always run in release builds rather than being silently skipped. This may help prevent accidentally shipping releases without proper security hardening enabled.

title: scripted-diff: rename libmultiprocess repository
summary: Renames references to the libmultiprocess repository in preparation for moving it to the bitcoin-core organization.
potential_rationale: This appears to be organizational housekeeping that may help consolidate Bitcoin Core related repositories under a single organization, likely improving project management and visibility.

title: build: don't show ccache summary with MSVC
summary: Prevents ccache summary information from being displayed when building with MSVC compiler.
potential_rationale: This likely fixes misleading build configuration output on Windows builds using MSVC, where ccache isn't actually being used. This may help developers avoid confusion about their build setup.

title: ci: use LLVM 20.1.0 for MSAN
summary: Updates the continuous integration system to use the official LLVM 20.1.0 release instead of the release candidate version for Memory Sanitizer (MSAN) testing.
potential_rationale: This likely ensures more stable and reliable testing infrastructure by using the final LLVM release, which may help catch memory-related bugs more effectively during development.

title: doc: link to benchcoin over bitcoinperf
summary: Updates documentation to link to the benchcoin repository instead of bitcoinperf for Bitcoin Core benchmarking tools.

title: crypto: disable ASan for sha256_sse4 with Clang
